OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: asOfDate
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: As of Date
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: The effective date of the data in the file. In other words, the date when the record was created or last updated in the source system. When time is not specified in the field, it will default to T00:00:00.000Z

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: amountPaidOnBuildingClaim
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Amount Paid on Building Claim
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Dollar amount paid on the building claim. In some instances, a negative amount may appear which occurs when a check issued to a policy holder is not cashed and has to be re-issued

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: amountPaidOnContentsClaim
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Amount Paid on Contents Claim
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Dollar amount paid on the contents claim. In some instances, a negative amount may appear, which occurs when a check issued to a policy holder is not cashed and has to be re-issued

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: amountPaidOnIncreasedCostOfComplianceClaim
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Amount Paid on Increased Cost of Compliance (ICC) Claim
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: ICC coverage is one of several flood insurance resources for policyholders who need additional help rebuilding after a flood. It provides up to $30,000 to help cover the cost of mitigation measures that will reduce the flood risk

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: netBuildingPaymentAmount
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Net Building Payment Amount
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Net building payment amount made to insured in dollars

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: netContentsPaymentAmount
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Net Contents Payment Amount
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Net contents payment amount made to insured in dollars

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: agricultureStructureIndicator
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Agriculture Structure Indicator
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Indicates whether a building is reported as being an agricultural structure in the policy application. Yes is indicated with either a 'true' or '1'. No is indicated with either a 'false' or '0'

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: basementEnclosureCrawlspaceType
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Basement Enclosure Crawlspace Type

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: policyCount
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Policy Count
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Insured units in an active status. A policy contract ceases to be in an active status as of the cancellation date or the expiration date. Residential Condominium Building Association Policy (RCBAP) contracts are stored as a single policy contract but insure multiple units and therefore represent multiple policies

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: crsClassificationCode
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: CRS Classification Code

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: dateOfLoss
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Date of Loss
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Date on which water first entered the insured building. When time is not specified in the field, it will default to T00:00:00.000Z

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: elevatedBuildingIndicator
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Elevated Building Indicator
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description:
Indicates whether a building meets the NFIP definition of an elevated building. Yes is indicated with either a 'true' or '1'. No is indicated with either a 'false' or '0'. An elevated building is a no-basement building that was constructed so as to meet the following criteria: 
1. The top of the elevated floor (all A zones) or the bottom of the lowest horizontal structural member of the lowest floor (all V zones) is above ground level. 
2. The building is adequately anchored. 
3. The method of elevation is pilings, columns (posts and piers), shear walls (not in V zones), or solid foundation perimeter walls (not in V zones).

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: elevationCertificateIndicator
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Elevation Certificate Indicator

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: elevationDifference
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Elevation Difference
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description:
Difference between the elevation of the lowest floor used for rating, or the floodproofed elevation, and the base flood elevation (BFE), or base flood depth, as appropriate. Round to nearest higher elevation difference in whole feet using .5 as the midpoint

NOTE: The depth shown on the Flood Insurance Rate Map (FIRM) for Zone AO that indicates the depth of water above the highest adjacent grade resulting from a flood that has a 1% chance of equaling or exceeding that level in any given year.The elevation of surface water resulting from a flood that has a 1% chance of equaling or exceeding that level in any given year. The BFE is shown on the FloodInsurance Rate Map (FIRM) forZones AE, AH, A1-A30, AR, AR/A, AR/AE, AR/A1-A30, AR/AH, AR/A0, V1-V30 andVE.
The value of 9999.0 indicates the field is not reported and/or used for this policy

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: baseFloodElevation
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Base Flood Elevation
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Base Flood Elevation (BFE) is the elevation  in feet from the Elevation Certificate at which there is a 1% chance per year of flooding.

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: ratedFloodZone
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Rated Flood Zone

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: houseWorship
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: House of Worship
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Indicates whether a building is reported as being a house of worship in the policy application. Yes is indicated with either a 'true' or '1'. No is indicated with either a 'false' or '0'

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: locationOfContents
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Location of Contents

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: lowestAdjacentGrade
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Lowest Adjacent Grade
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Lowest natural grade adjacent to the insured structure prior to excavating or filling. The difference in feet of the lowest natural grade adjacent to the building from the reference level of the building

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: lowestFloorElevation
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Lowest Floor Elevation
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: A building's lowest floor is the floor or level (including basement/enclosure/crawlspace/subgrade crawlspace) that is used as the point of reference when rating a building. This includes the level to which a building is floodproofed. The elevation in feet of the reference level of the building from the Elevation Certificate.

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: numberOfFloorsInTheInsuredBuilding
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Number of Floors in the Insured Building

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: nonProfitIndicator
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Non-Profit Indicator
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Indicates whether a building is reported as being a non-profit in the policy application. Yes is indicated with either a 'true' or '1'. No is indicated with either a 'false' or '0'

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: obstructionType
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Obstruction Type
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description:
Code that describes the type of obstruction that may be present under an elevated building. An obstruction includes an enclosed area and/or machinery and equipment attached to the building below the lowest elevated floor.
10 : Free of obstruction
15 : With obstruction: enclosure/crawlspace with proper openings not used for rating (not applicable in V Zones)
20 : With obstruction: less than 300 sq. ft. with breakaway walls, but no machinery or equipment attached to building below lowest elevated floor, or the elevation of the machinery or equipment is at or above the Base Flood Elevation
24 : With obstruction: less than 300 sq. ft. with breakaway walls or finished enclosure and with machinery or equipment attached to building below lowest elevated floor. The elevation of the machinery or equipment is below the Base Flood Elevation.
30 : With obstruction: 300 sq. ft. or more with breakaway walls, but no machinery or equipment attached to building below the Base Flood Elevation
34 : With obstruction: 300 sq. ft. or more with breakaway walls or finished enclosure and with machinery or equipment attached to building below the Base Flood Elevation
40 : With obstruction: no walls, but the elevation of machinery or equipment attached to building is below Base Flood Elevation
50 : With obstruction: nonbreakawaywalls/crawlspace or finished enclosure with no machinery or equipment attached to building below lowest elevated floor
54 : With obstruction: nonbreakawaywalls/crawlspace or finished enclosure with machinery or equipment attached to building below lowest elevated floor
60 : With obstruction
70 : With Certification subgrade crawlspace (AE, A01-A30, unnumbered A, AO, AH,AOB, AHB zones)
80 : Without Certification subgrade crawlspace (all zones)
90 : With Enclosure: Elevated buildings with elevator below the BFE in A zones. No other enclosure below the BFE.
91 : Free of Obstruction: Elevated buildings with lattice, slats, or shutters (including louvers) enclosing the elevator below the BFE in V zones. No other obstruction or machinery and equipment (M&E) servicing the building located below the BFE. The building will be rated free of obstruction with the V zone elevator loading added to the building basic limit.
92 : With Enclosure: Elevated buildings with elevator below the BFE in A zones. Enclosure below the BFE has no proper openings, is finished, or is used for other than parking, building access,or storage.
94 : With Obstruction: Elevated buildings with elevator below the BFE in V zones. No other obstruction or machinery and equipment (M&E)servicing the building located belowthe BFE.
95 : With Obstruction: Elevated buildings with elevator below the BFE in V zones. Breakaway wall obstruction is unfinished and elevator and obstruction are located below the BFE but no machinery and equipment (M&E) servicing the building is located below the BFE.
96 : With Obstruction: Elevated buildings with elevator below the BFE in V zones. Breakaway wall obstruction is finished or is used for other than parking, building access, or storage.
97 : With Obstruction: Elevated buildings with elevator below the BFE in V zones. No other obstruction, but has M&E servicing the building located below the BFE.
98 : With Obstruction: Elevated buildings with elevator below the BFE in V zones. Breakaway walls obstruction and M&E servicing the building are located below the BFE.

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: occupancyType
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Occupancy Type

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: originalConstructionDate
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Original Construction Date
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: The original date of the construction of the building.

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: originalNBDate
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Original NB Date
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: The original date of the flood policy.

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: postFIRMConstructionIndicator
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Post-FIRM Construction Indicator
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Indicates whether construction was started before or after publication of the FIRM. Yes is indicated with either a 'true' or '1'. No is indicated with either a 'false' or '0'. For insurance rating purposes, buildings for which the start of construction or substantial improvement was after December 31, 1974, or on or after the effective date of the initial FIRM for the community, whichever is later, are considered Post-FIRM construction.

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: rateMethod
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Rate Method
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Indicates policy rating method: 1 - Manual; 2 - Specific; 3 - Alternative; 4 - V-Zone Risk Factor Rating Form; 5 - Underinsured Condominium Master Policy; 6 - Provisional; 7 - Preferred Risk Policy (PRPs issued for eligible properties located within a non-Special Flood Hazard Area [non-SFHA]); 8 - Tentative; 9 - MPPP Policy; A - Optional Post-1981 V Zone; B - Pre-FIRM policies with elevation rating - Flood Insurance Manual rate tables; E - FEMA Pre-FIRM Special Rates; F - Leased Federal Property; G - Group Flood Insurance Policy (GFIP); P - Preferred Risk Policy (A PRP renewal issued in the first year following a map revision for an eligible property that was newly mapped into the SFHA by the map revision, or new business written for an eligible property that was newly mapped into the SFHA by a map revision effective on or after October 1, 2008 - PRP Eligibility Extension); Q - Preferred Risk Policy (subsequent PRP renewals where the previous policy year was reported as a 'P' or 'Q'); R - Newly Mapped into SFHA; S - FEMA Special Rates; T - Severe Repetitive Loss Properties (formerly Target Group Full Risk). Effective October 1, 2013, code will no longer be valid; W - Pre-FIRM policies with elevation rating - Submit-for-Rate procedures;RatingEngine : Risk Rated policies where FEMA calculates the rates based on various risk factors.

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: smallBusinessIndicatorBuilding
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Small Business Indicator Building
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Indicates whether or not the insured represents a small business. Yes is indicated with either a 'true' or '1'. No is indicated with either a 'false' or '0'. Small business is defined as a business with fewer than 100 employees in the policy application.

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: totalBuildingInsuranceCoverage
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Total Building Insurance Coverage
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Total Insurance Amount in whole dollars on the Building

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: totalContentsInsuranceCoverage
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Total Contents Insurance Coverage
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Total Insurance Amount in whole dollars on the Contents

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: yearOfLoss
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Year of Loss
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Year in which the flood loss occurred

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: primaryResidenceIndicator
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Primary Residence Indicator
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Indicates whether a building is a primary residence. Yes is indicated with either a 'true' or '1'. No is indicated with either a 'false' or '0'. A primary residence is a single family building, condominium unit, apartment unit, or unit within a cooperative building that will be lived in by the policyholder or the policyholder's spouse for: More than 50% of the 365 calendar days following the current policy effective date; or 50% or less of the 365 calendar days following the current policy effective date if the policyholder has only one residence and does not lease that residence to another party or use it as rental or income property at any time during the policy term. A policyholder and the policyholder's spouse may not collectively have more than one primary residence

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: buildingDamageAmount
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Building Damage Amount
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: The actual cash value amount of damage to a main property in whole dollars.

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: buildingDeductibleCode
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Building Deductible Code

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: buildingPropertyValue
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Building Property Value
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: The actual cash value of the main building in whole dollars as estimated by an adjuster

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: causeOfDamage
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Cause of Damage Code

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: condominiumCoverageTypeCode
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Condominium Coverage Type Code

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: contentsDamageAmount
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Contents Damage Amount
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: The actual cash value amount of damage to contents in whole dollars

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: contentsDeductibleCode
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Contents Deductible Code

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: contentsPropertyValue
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Contents Property Value
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: The actual cash value of the contents in whole dollars as estimated by an adjuster

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: disasterAssistanceCoverageRequired
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Disaster Assistance Coverage Required Code

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: eventDesignationNumber
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Event Designation Number (EDN)
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description:
The Event Designation Number (EDN) is a unique identifier established for a catastrophe flood event.
NOTE: EDN has replaced FICO as the new event tracking designation. Prior to June 2020 flood events were assigned FICO number designations; after June 2020, flood events are assigned EDN number designations. A public bulletin will designate the EDN number assignments by state with corresponding dates of loss. Example: 2 letter state abbreviation (TX), 2-digit event designator (02) and 2-digit year (20). Full example would be TX0220.

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: ficoNumber
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Flood Insurance Claims Office (FICO) Number
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description:
Number assigned at the time of a significant flooding event to uniquely identify each affected state. A public bulletin will designate the FICO number assignments by state with corresponding dates of loss. 
Note: Flood Insurance Claims Office (FICO) is an NFIP claims processing office set up in a catastrophe area when a sufficient number of flood claims result from a single event. EDN has replaced FICO as the new event tracking designation. Prior to June 2020 flood events were assigned FICO number designations; after June 2020, flood events are assigned EDN number designations. A public bulletin will designate the EDN number assignments by state with corresponding dates of loss. Example: 2 letter state abbreviation (TX), 2-digit event designator (02) and 2-digit year (20). Full example would be TX0220.

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: floodCharacteristicsIndicator
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Flood Characteristics Indicator

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: floodWaterDuration
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Flood Water Duration
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Number of hours flood water remained in the insured building

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: floodproofedIndicator
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Floodproofed Indicator
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description:
Indicates if the insured structure is floodproofed. Yes is indicated with either a 'true' or '1'. No is indicated with either a 'false' or '0'. 
NOTE: Floodproofing is any combination of structural and non-structural additions, changes, or adjustments to structures which reduce or eliminate flood damage to a property. Floodproofing may be an alternative to elevating a building to or above the BFE; however, the NFIP requires a Floodproofing Certificate prior to considering floodproofing mitigation measures in rating a structure

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: floodEvent
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Flood Event
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Name given to a flooding catastrophe

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: iccCoverage
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Increased Cost of Compliance (ICC) Coverage
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description:
Amount of Increased Cost of Compliance (ICC) coverage for building in whole dollars.
NOTE: ICC coverage is the coverage for expenses that a property owner must incur, above and beyond the cost to repair the physical damage the building actually sustained from a flooding event, to comply with mitigation requirements of state or local floodplain management ordinances or laws. Acceptable mitigation measures are elevation, floodproofing, relocation, demolition, or any combination thereof

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: netIccPaymentAmount
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Net Increased Cost of Compliance (ICC) Payment Amount
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Net Increased Cost of Compliance (ICC) payment amount made to insured in dollars.

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: nfipRatedCommunityNumber
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Rated NFIP Community Number
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Asix-digit designation identifying the rated NFIP community. The first two numbers are the state code. The next four are the FEMA-assigned community number. An alphabetical suffix is added to a community number to identify revisions in the FIRM for that community

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: nfipCommunityNumberCurrent
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Current NFIP Community Number
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Asix-digit designation identifying the current NFIP community. The first two numbers are the state code. The next four are the FEMA-assigned community number. An alphabetical suffix is added to a community number to identify revisions in the FIRM for that community

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: nfipCommunityName
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Rated NFIP Community Name
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Alocalpoliticalentitythat has the authority to adopt and enforce floodplain ordinances for the area under its jurisdiction.

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: nonPaymentReasonContents
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Contents Non-Payment Reason

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: nonPaymentReasonBuilding
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Building Non-Payment Reason

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: numberOfUnits
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Number of Units
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: The number of residential and nonresidential units covered by the Condominium Master Policy

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: buildingReplacementCost
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Building Replacement Cost
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description:
Estimated cost in whole dollars to replace the building as reported by the insurer. 
NOTE : Replacement cost is not market value, and it does not include the cost of the land or contents. It is strictly the estimated cost to reconstruct the building

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: contentsReplacementCost
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Contents Replacement Cost
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description:
Estimated cost in whole dollars to replace the contents as reported by the insurer. 
NOTE : Replacement cost is not market value, and it does not include the cost of the land or building. It is strictly the estimated cost to replace the contents.

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: replacementCostBasis
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Replacement Cost Basis

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: stateOwnedIndicator
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: State-Owned Indicator
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Indicates whether insured property is state owned. Yes is indicated with either a 'true' or '1'. No is indicated with either a 'false' or '0'.  NOTE: State owned property is land or other assets that are legally owned by  the state

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: waterDepth
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Water Depth
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Depth of flood water in inches. Note: there are instances where measurements were provided in feet.

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: floodZoneCurrent
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Current Flood Zone

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: buildingDescriptionCode
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Building Description Code

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: rentalPropertyIndicator
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Rental Property Indicator
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Indicates if the property is a rental property. Yes is indicated with either a 'true' or '1'. No is indicated with either a 'false' or '0'. NOTE: A rental property is a property from which the owner receives payment from the occupant(s), known as tenants, in return for occupying or using the property. Rental properties may be either residential or commercial

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: state
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: State
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: The two-character alpha abbreviation of the state in which the insured property is located

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: reportedCity
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Reported City
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: This is the city of the insured property as reported to us by our Write Your Own (WYO) partners

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: reportedZipCode
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Reported Zip Code
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: 5-digit Postal Zip Code of the insured property as reported by WYO partners

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: countyCode
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: County Code
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: FIPS code uniquely identifying the primary County (e.g., 011 represents Broward County) associated with the project. Note, the County Code field may not reflect the individual county the property is located as projects can be associated with more than one county

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: censusTract
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Census Tract
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: U.S. Census Bureau defined census tracts; statistical subdivisions of a county or equivalent entity that are updated prior to each decennial census. The NFIP relies on our geocoding service to assign 11-digit census tract codes

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: censusBlockGroupFips
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Census Block Group FIPS
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: The first digit of the census block number identifies the block group. Block numbers beginning with a zero (in Block Group 0) are associated with water-only areas. Typically, Block Groups have a population of 600 to 3,000 people.

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: latitude
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Latitude
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Approximate latitude of the insured building (to 1 decimal place). This represents the approximate location of the insured property. The precision has been lessened to ensure individual privacy. This may result in a point location that exists in an incorrect county or state. Use the state and county fields for record aggregation for these dimensions

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: longitude
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: Longitude
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Approximate longitude of the insured building (to 1 decimal place). This represents the approximate location of the insured property. The precision has been lessened to ensure individual privacy. This may result in a point location that exists in an incorrect county or state. Use the state and county fields for record aggregation for these dimensions

OpenFemaDataSetFields.name: id
OpenFemaDataSetFields.title: ID
OpenFemaDataSetFields.description: Unique ID assigned to the record
